title: N,N' -Dipyridylthiourea as a new sensitive reagent for the extraction and spectrophotometric determination of osmium(VI) and iridium(III)
author: S. Palchaudhury (Mukhopadhyay)
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Geology Department, Presidency College, Kolkata-700 073, India 
 E-mail  : s_palchaudhuri@vsnl.net 
 Manuscript received 8 July 2002, revised 31 December 2002, accepted 4 March 2003  

 N,N'-Dipyridylthiourea (DPyT) reacts with Os VI  and Ir Ill  forming coloured extractable complexes  ( λ  max   355 nm in chloroform : ethanol for Os complex and 335 nm for Ir complex in the same extractant). In presence of moderate excess of diverse ions both Os and Ir can be determined with DPyT in ppm level. The molar extinction coefficient and Sandell's sensitivity values for Os VI  and Ir III  complexes are 3.05 x 10 4 , 0.006 and 1.89 x 10 4  dm 3  mol -1  cm -1  and 0.01  µ g cm -2 , respectively. The Os and Ir complexes have 1 : 2 and 1 : 3 M : R stoichiometries respectively.
